prompt:
You are an architecture reviewer for a VS Code extension (the "Dabbler AI
Orchestration" Work Explorer). Two design decisions for Session 2 of the
release-boundary set (Sets 091-094 ship together) need a ruling BEFORE coding.
Answer each tersely with a SINGLE concrete recommendation; flag anything that
would be Critical/Major as proposed.

## Shared, non-negotiable context (do NOT re-litigate)

- Adjudication A: `docs/modules.yaml` is created ONLY on explicit user actions,
  never on activation / passive refresh. The shared primitive
  `ensureModulesManifest(root)` (atomic exclusive-create of the canonical
  `MODULES_YAML_TEMPLATE`, skip-existing, never overwrites invalid) already
  exists and shipped in Session 1.
- The house "copy-prompt" pattern (src/commands/copyPromptCommands.ts,
  wizard/sessionGenPrompt.ts): a command builds a POINTER-STYLE prompt (names
  repo-relative file paths, never embeds file contents) and writes it to the
  clipboard via `vscode.env.clipboard.writeText`.
- The webview form posts typed `gettingStartedAction` messages
  (open-folder / build-structure / open-modules) on a channel SEPARATE from the
  `executeCommand` allowlist; the Define-modules section currently has ONE
  button ("Open modules.yaml") wired to the `open-modules` action.

## DECISION 1 — Parallel-session-sets shelving + escape hatch (operator-mandated)

Operator verdict amendment 7: "Remove the 'Create parallel session sets where
possible' checkbox + its prompt guidance from primary UI; KEEP the
`prerequisites:` machinery and the worktree tooling; preserve an advanced path
(a Command-Palette variant OR a `parallelHint` setting) for the narrow
multiple-branches-in-one-module case. UI-level shelving, reversible."

Current state after Session 1:
- The form checkbox is already removed. In `wizard/sessionGenPrompt.ts`,
  `buildSessionGenPrompt(bundle, options)` still supports `options.parallel`,
  which injects a `PARALLEL_GUIDANCE` block (decompose for concurrency; declare
  ordering with `prerequisites:`; sets without prerequisites run in parallel
  worktrees). After S1 NO caller sets `parallel:true` (the form was the only
  one; the palette command `dabbler.generateSessionSetPrompt` and the Set-093
  `ai-sets` module row action both pass no parallel flag). So `PARALLEL_GUIDANCE`
  is currently dead-but-present.
- SEPARATE and OUT OF SCOPE (keep untouched): the `prerequisites:` spec field;
  the worktree tooling; and the per-EXISTING-set "start next PARALLEL session"
  copy commands (`dabblerSessionSets.copyStartCommand.parallel`,
  `dabbler.copyStartNextParallelSessionPrompt`) — those concern running a second
  worktree session of one set, a different feature from "parallel session SETS".

The two escape-hatch options:
  (1a) Command-Palette variant: a new command
       `dabbler.generateParallelSessionSetPrompt` that calls
       `copySessionSetGenPrompt(context, { parallel: true })`. Per-invocation
       intent, no sticky state, no config surface; one near-duplicate command;
       does NOT compose with the module row-action path (palette-only).
  (1b) `parallelHint` setting: a workspace config
       `dabblerSessionSets.parallelHint` (boolean, default false). When true,
       `copySessionSetGenPrompt` injects `PARALLEL_GUIDANCE` on EVERY
       decomposition path (palette + row action) uniformly. Discoverable in
       Settings; persists per-workspace; but sticky state the operator must
       remember to turn off, and it silently changes every decomposition prompt
       while enabled.

Q1. Which escape hatch — (1a) Command-Palette variant, (1b) `parallelHint`
    setting, or a hybrid? Give ONE recommendation and the decisive reason,
    given the operator framed the case as "narrow / occasional".
Q2. After choosing, does `PARALLEL_GUIDANCE` / the `options.parallel` parameter
    STAY in `sessionGenPrompt.ts` (fed only by the escape hatch), or should any
    of it be deleted? What is the minimal, prefer-removal shape? Name the
    explicit regression test that pins "`prerequisites:` machinery + worktrees
    untouched" so the shelving cannot be misread as a removal of the mechanism.

## DECISION 2 — D6 module-decomposition copy-prompt command surface

New command `dabbler.copyModuleDecompositionPrompt` (house copy-prompt pattern):
its prompt instructs an AI agent to analyze the PROJECT, decompose it into
MODULES, and write `docs/modules.yaml` (slug, title, codeRoots, planPath,
integration `touches`), honoring the invariants (globally-unique session-set
names; `module` is a grouping attribute, never identity). It is the FOURTH
ensure-write site: it must call `ensureModulesManifest(root)` (create-if-absent
canonical template) so the AI has the scaffold to fill in. It must be
"referenced from the Define-modules section copy AND the manifest template's
header comment."

IMPORTANT distinction: the existing Set-093 `ai-sets` module row action already
copies a module-targeted SESSION-SET decomposition prompt (decompose a plan ->
session sets). D6 is a DIFFERENT prompt: decompose a PROJECT -> modules, writing
the manifest. Do not conflate them.

Surface options:
  (2a) Form button + palette command: add a second button ("Copy AI
       decomposition prompt") to the Define-modules section, posting a NEW
       `copy-decomposition-prompt` gettingStartedAction -> host runs the D6
       flow (ensure manifest + copy). ALSO register
       `dabbler.copyModuleDecompositionPrompt` for the palette + the manifest
       header reference. Most discoverable at the exact onboarding moment;
       costs one new protocol action id + client.js wiring + a host handler.
  (2b) Palette command only, referenced by TEXT in the Define-modules section
       copy (e.g. "run 'Dabbler: Copy Module Decomposition Prompt' from the
       Command Palette"). No new protocol surface; but poor discoverability —
       the operator must know to open the palette.

Q3. Which surface — (2a) form button + palette, (2b) palette-only + text
    reference? Give ONE recommendation weighing onboarding discoverability vs.
    added protocol/webview surface, noting the section already owns one form
    button ("Open modules.yaml") so the wiring pattern exists.
Q4. Where exactly should the `ensureModulesManifest` call live for the D6 copy
    action so it stays an EXPLICIT-ACTION write (never a passive path), and is
    there any hazard in ensuring-then-copying vs copying a prompt that itself
    tells the AI to create the file? Confirm ordering (ensure before building
    the prompt so the pointer never dangles).
Q5. The prompt is pointer-style. Confirm it should REFERENCE the manifest path
    + the project plan path rather than embed contents, and name the minimum
    set of invariants the prompt body must state so a fresh AI agent produces a
    schema-valid `docs/modules.yaml`.
content:
## Q1 — Escape hatch: **(1a) Command-Palette variant.**
"Narrow/occasional" == per-invocation intent. **(1b) is Major-as-proposed:** it is workspace-sticky state that, while enabled, silently injects `PARALLEL_GUIDANCE` into the *common* decomposition path (row action + palette); an operator who forgets to toggle it off contaminates every subsequent normal prompt. (1a) scopes the guidance to one deliberate invocation and keeps the row-action/common path provably clean. Do not build the hybrid — operator framed it as `OR`.

## Q2 — **Keep `options.parallel` + `PARALLEL_GUIDANCE`; delete nothing further.**
With 1a chosen, `dabbler.generateParallelSessionSetPrompt` → `copySessionSetGenPrompt(ctx, { parallel: true })` → `buildSessionGenPrompt(bundle, { parallel: true })` becomes the *sole live feeder*, so the parameter/block are no longer dead code.

Minimal prefer-removal shape: one boolean `options.parallel` gating one `PARALLEL_GUIDANCE` const, reachable **only** via the new command — no config, no form/client wiring, no second code path. Verify no other branch reads `parallel`.

Regression pin — `wizard/__tests__/sessionGenPrompt.parallelShelving.test.ts`:
- `buildSessionGenPrompt(bundle)` (default) → **omits** `PARALLEL_GUIDANCE` **but still emits the `prerequisites:` field/doc line** (mechanism intact, UI shelved).
- `buildSessionGenPrompt(bundle, { parallel: true })` → **includes** `PARALLEL_GUIDANCE` (hatch works).
- Registry assertion: `dabblerSessionSets.copyStartCommand.parallel` and `dabbler.copyStartNextParallelSessionPrompt` remain registered.

The default-prompt-still-documents-`prerequisites:` assertion is the one that pins "shelving ≠ removal."

## Q3 — Surface: **(2a) form button + palette.**
The Define-modules section already owns a `gettingStartedAction` button ("Open modules.yaml"), so 2a is *additive to an existing pattern* — new action id `copy-decomposition-prompt` + one `client.js` case + one host handler — not a new mechanism. Writing the manifest is a first-run onboarding step; 2b's palette-only path is undiscoverable at that exact moment (requires foreknowledge). Register `dabbler.copyModuleDecompositionPrompt` for the palette regardless — the manifest-header reference needs it. Not Critical/Major: it's a typed action on the separate channel, not the `executeCommand` allowlist.

## Q4 — `ensureModulesManifest` lives in the **shared D6 command body**, invoked only by the button handler + palette command.
Both entry points call one `runCopyModuleDecompositionPrompt(ctx)` executing, in order: (1) `ensureModulesManifest(root)` → (2) build pointer prompt → (3) `clipboard.writeText`. Reachable only from a user gesture, so the write stays explicit-action per Adjudication A.

**Flag Critical-as-proposed:** do NOT hoist `ensureModulesManifest` into any webview render / activation / refresh path — that converts it to a passive create and violates Adjudication A.

Ordering: **ensure BEFORE building the prompt** — confirmed — so the repo-relative pointer to `docs/modules.yaml` never dangles.

Ensure-then-copy vs a prompt that tells the AI to create the file → **prefer ensure-then-copy.** Delegating creation to the AI (a) dangles the pointer at copy time and (b) lets the AI author a non-canonical scaffold that bypasses the primitive's atomic exclusive-create / skip-existing / never-overwrite-invalid guarantees. The extension owns scaffold creation; the AI's job is FILL-IN, not create. Creating the canonical template on copy even if never pasted is acceptable — explicit gesture, inert idempotent template.

## Q5 — Pointer-style: **confirmed — reference, do not embed.**
Reference repo-relative `docs/modules.yaml` (the just-ensured scaffold to fill) + the repo-relative project plan path; embed neither's contents.

Minimum invariants the prompt body must state for a schema-valid result:
1. Session-set names are **globally unique** (across all modules), not per-module.
2. `module` is a **grouping attribute, never identity** — a set is identified by its unique name; module only groups.
3. Per-module required fields: `slug` (unique), `title`, `codeRoots`, `planPath`, `integration.touches` — all paths **repo-relative**.
4. **Fill the existing `docs/modules.yaml` in place**; preserve the canonical template keys/shape; do not recreate or rename.
5. Emit **valid YAML** conforming to the template structure.
